B.Tech. 4
th
 Semester 
 
END-SEMESTER EXAMINATION, APRIL-MAY 2025 
 
Max. Marks: 50    Course Title: Electronic Design Workshop 
Duration: 3 Hours    Course Code: ECECC402, EIECC402                       
Note: - Attempt all questions in the given order only. Missing data/information (if any), may 
be suitably assumed & mentioned in the answer. 
 
Page 1 of 3 
 
 
Q. No. Question Marks CO 
Q 1 Attempt any 2 parts of the following 
 
  
1a Explain the various types of triggering modes and their 
use/applications in a modern digital storage oscilloscope (DSO). 
 
5 CO 1 
1b A DSO is set up with a horizontal setting of 200 uS/div and the 
vertical setting of 1V/div. The ground reference is set to be in the 
middle of the DSO screen.  The DSO has 10 horizontal and 
vertical graticules. Draw images of the DSO screens to show 
how the following waveforms will appear in DC and AC 
coupling modes: 
 
● A Sine wave of 1 KHz frequency and 2V pk-pk signal 
with a 1V DC offset voltage. 
● A 0 to 5V PWM signal of 1KHz frequency and with 20% 
duty cycle. 
 
5 CO 1 
1c What is the meaning of ‘3-½ digits’ in a 3-½ digit digital 
multimeter (DMM)? Explain the operation of a 3-½ digit DMM 
with the help of a suitable block diagram for DC/AC Voltage, 
DC current and resistance measurement.  
 
5 CO 1 
Q 2 Attempt any 2 parts of the following 
 
  
2a Refer to the circuit diagram in figure 1. The signal f1 is a 
sinusoidal waveform of 100 Hz and 1V pk-pk and f2 is a 
sinusoidal waveform of 2 KHz and 0.1V pk-pk. 
R1=R2=R3=10KOhms. 
 
● Draw the waveform appearing at the output of the 
op-amp. 
● Design a circuit (with component values) to extract f1 
and f2 signals from the op-amp output. 
 
5 CO 2 
2b Refer to the circuit diagram in figure 2. CLK signal is a 0 to 5V 
PWM signal with 10% duty cycle and 1KHz frequency. The 
5 CO 2 

 
Page 2 of 3 
 
 
DFF and the rest of the circuit has a Vcc of 5V. The LED 
forward voltage drop is 2V. The BJT has a 훃 = 500 and 
VCE(Sat) = 0.1V. R1 = 100 Ohms and R2 = 2.9 Ohms.  The 
DFF V(OH) = 3.5V, V(IH)= 3V V(OL) = 0.4V, V(IL)=0.8V. 
Vbe(Sat) = 0.9V.  Estimate the following: 
 
1. The rate at which the LED is being turned on and off. 
2. The Peak current through the LED. 
3. The average current through the LED. 
 
2c What are the various ways of prototyping an electronic circuit? 
 
5 CO 2 
Q 3 Attempt any 2 parts of the following 
 
  
3a A semiconductor temperature sensor provides 10mV/°C and 
outputs 0V at 0 °C. For a sensing range of 0 to 100 °C, design an 
amplifier to match the Arduino ADC working in the 0 to 5V 
range with 10-bit resolution. If the temperature sensed by the 
sensor is 10 °C, what would be the output of the ADC? 
 
5 CO 3 
3b Design a 5V output Zener regulator circuit to supply 50 mA 
(max) current to the load. The input voltage varies between 8V 
and 12V. Calculate the required series resistor value and power 
rating, Zener power rating and maximum output voltage 
variation for (a) input voltage variation and (b) Output load 
current variation. Zener knee current I(zk)=1 mA and dynamic 
resistance of zener r(d) = 3 Ohms. 
 
5 CO 3 
3c What are the steps involved in designing a PCB from start to 
finish? 
 
5 CO 3 
Q 4 Attempt any 2 parts of the following 
 
  
4a What is Pulse Width Modulation? Design a circuit using an 
Arduino to control the intensity of an LED using PWM and an 
external switch to allow a maximum of 100 mA current through 
the LED. 
 
5 CO 4 
4b Refer to figure 3 which shows a microphone with a biasing 
resistor. The output of this circuit is 10mV pk-pk AC voltage 
riding on 4V of DC. Design a microphone output amplifier 
circuit to match the Arduino ADC working in the 0 to 5V range 
with 10-bit resolution. What will be the ADC value when there 
is pin drop silence? 
 
5 CO 4 
4c Design an Arduino circuit to control a fan working at 220V AC. 
The Arduino has a pushbutton which can be pressed to toggle 
the fan. Draw the entire circuit and explain its operation. 
 
5 CO 4 

 
 
Figure 1.      Figure 2. 
 
 
Figure 3 
 
Page 3 of 3 
 
 
Q 5 Attempt any 2 parts of the following 
 
  
5a Explain the operation of a 3D printer and how it is useful for 
electronic system prototyping. 
 
5 CO 5 
5b What are the various communication protocols and what are 
their uses and applications? 
 
5 CO 5 
5c Design a mains operated power supply to provide +12V and 
-12V regulated supply voltage at a maximum current of 250 mA. 
 
5 CO 5
